steps.title: Keep rejects as the main verb
steps.content: The main subject is "Popper's theory," and the main verb is "rejects" near the end of the sentence. The blank must not add another finite main verb before "rejects."

steps.title: Use a participle modifier
steps.content: Choice A, Choice B, and Choice D are finite verb forms, so each would create an uncoordinated second main verb. A present participle can instead modify "theory" while leaving "rejects" as the sentence's main verb. The answer is C.
